that eagerness of mind in Mrs. Dashwood which must generally have led to imprudence.

definitions.english: not able to be undone or altered; not reversible

definitions.english: to strengthen or support physically or mentally; to undergo or suffet; to keep in existence

words.en: giving rise or likely to give rise to public disagreement

definitions.english: giving rise or likely to give rise to public disagreement

examples.english: Although several scholars have dated Beowulf to the late ninth through early eleventh centuries, others have argued that doing so privileges a controversial interpretation of the social conditions of the period.

words.en: able to resume normal shape after being stretched or compressed; flexible

definitions.english: able to resume normal shape after being stretched or compressed; flexible

petroleum-based fibers that are often used in fabrics.

words.en: to put forward as a hypothesis; to form a tentative theory

definitions.english: to put forward as a hypothesis; to form a tentative theory

examples.english: A team of political scientists hypothesized that municipalities are much more likely to respond to firms and offer incentives if expansions can be announced in time to benefit local elected officials than if they can't.

merged_lines: n. 极度痛苦；剧痛 (severe mental or physical pain or suffering)
真题例句：Poetry, which brings together or separates, which fortifies or brings anguish, which
shores up or demolishes souls, which gives or robs men of faith and vigor, is more necessary to a
people than industry itself, for industry provides them with a means of subsistence, while
literature gives them the desire and strength for life.
中文释义：诗歌，它使人相聚或分离，使人坚强或带来痛苦，它支撑或摧毁灵魂，它给
予或剥夺人的信仰与活力，对于一个民族而言，它比工业更力必要，因为工业提供生存
的手段，而文学则赋予他们生活的渴望和力量。

words.en: a covering suspended over a throne or bed; the uppermost layer of branches in a forest

definitions.english: a covering suspended over a throne or bed; the uppermost layer of branches in a forest

examples.english: O thou pure sunlight, and thou air, earth's canopy, how often have ye heard the strains of my lament the wild blows dealt against this bleeding breast, when dark night fails!
examples.chinese: 啊，你这纯净的阳光，还有你，大地的天篷—空气，你们曾多少次听见我 哀伤的曲调，听见这流血胸膛遭受的疯狂打击，在黑夜消退之时！

memory: 源自希腊语 konopcion（蚊帐）。核心意象是"覆盖在上方的罩篷"。
words.cognates_text: canopied （adj.有罩盖的）。

merged_lines: n. 天篷；华盖；森林冠层 （a covering suspended over a throne or bed; the uppermost layer of
branches in a forest)
真题例句：O thou pure sunlight, and thou air, earth's canopy, how often have ye heard the
strains of my lament the wild blows dealt against this bleeding breast, when dark night fails!
中文释义：啊，你这纯净的阳光，还有你，大地的天篷—空气，你们曾多少次听见我
哀伤的曲调，听见这流血胸膛遭受的疯狂打击，在黑夜消退之时！

words.en: to produce and release a substance from a cell or gland; to conceal or hide

definitions.english: to produce and release a substance from a cell or gland; to conceal or hide

examples.english: Plant ecologists Anna Abrahao and Patricia de Britto Costa used microscopic analysis to determine that the roots of B. tomentose and B. macrantha, which grow directly into the quartzite, have clusters of fine hairs near the root tip; further analysis indicated that these hairs secrete both malic and citric acids.
examples.chinese: 植物生态学家安娜 •阿布拉昂和帕特里夏 •德布里托•科斯塔通过显微分析 确定，直接生长在石英岩中的 B.tomentose 和 B. macrantha 的根在根尖附近有簇状细 毛；进一步分析表明，这些细毛能分泌苹果酸和柠檬酸。

words.en: absolutely necessary or important; essential for life

definitions.chinese: 至关重要的；维持生命所必需的
definitions.english: absolutely necessary or important; essential for life

examples.english: The researchers hypothesize that the plants depend on dissolving underlying rock with these acids, as the process not only creates channels for continued growth but also releases phosphates that provide the vital nutrient phosphorus.
examples.chinese: 研究人员假设，这些植物依赖这些酸来溶解下方的岩石，因为该过程不仅为 持续生长创造了通道，还释放出提供关键养分磷的磷酸盐。

words.en: a picture, description, or imitation of a person or thing in which certain striking characteristics are exaggerated in order to create a comic or grotesque effect

words.en: to produce and discharge something, especially gas, light, heat, or sound

definitions.english: to produce and discharge something, especially gas, light, heat, or sound

examples.english: Although there is little genetic difference between river and cave A. mexicanus and all members of the species can emit the same sounds, biologist Carole Hyacinthe and colleagues found that the context and significance of those sounds vary by location —e.g., the click that river-dwelling A. mexicanus use to signal aggression is used by cave dwellers when foraging and the acoustic properties of cave fish sounds show some cave-specific variations as well.
examples.chinese: 尽管河流和洞穴中的墨西哥丽脂鲤之间基因差异很小，且所有成员都能发出 相同的声音，但生物学家卡罗尔•亚辛特及其同事发现，这些声音的背景和意义因地点 而异—例如，河流栖息的墨西哥丽脂鲤用来表示攻击的咔嗒声，被洞穴栖息者在觅食 时使用—而且洞穴鱼声音的声学特性也表现出一些洞穴特有的变异。

words.zh: 搏动；抽痛；有节奏的震动；搏动；抽痛；震颤
words.en: a strong, regular beat or sound; a feeling of pain in a seties of regular beats; to beat or sound with a strong, regular rhythm; to feel a series of sharp pains

definitions.chinese: 搏动；抽痛；有节奏的震动
definitions.english: a strong, regular beat or sound; a feeling of pain in a seties of regular beats
to beat or sound with a strong, regular rhythm; to feel a series of sharp pains

examples.english: "Then we sit and sob, and long for the gas-lit streets, and the sound of human voices, and the answering throb of human life."
examples.chinese: “然后我们坐着哭泣，渴望那些煤气灯照亮的街道，渴望人声，渴望那回应 着的人类生命的搏动。"

examples.english: By late afternoon my head was throbbing, and I couldn't see straight.
examples.chinese: 临近傍晚时我头痛欲裂，视线也开始模糊不清。

definitions.english: to hit or knock against something with force; to move with a jolting motion

examples.english: A study using obstructions that created large vortices in low-visibility conditions found that the narrow-headed lake sturgeon (Acipenser fulvescens) bumped into obstructions more often than the intermediate-headed dusky smooth-hound (Mustelus canis) did.
